confidence.positional_bb100: MEDIUM — corrected but has margin of error

leak_analysis.fix: Defend more vs c-bets with draws
Bet more for value, add river bluffs
Review for tilt, set session limits

concerning: Thursdays and Mondays are worst days (-17.65 and -13.44 bb/100)
After big losses, avg next session = -€12.54 (potential tilt)
Moving up stakes costs avg -€5.59 per session

period_comparison._description: Good period = Apr 5 to Jul 13 (100 days). Bad period = Jul 14 to Oct 27 (106 days). Split based on weekly P&L trend analysis.

target_stats_from_good_period._description: These are your stats from the good period. Use them as targets to get back to that level.

action_plan._description: Concrete steps to get back to good-period performance

immediate_fixes.target: Limp% from 12.2% back to 8.0% or less
immediate_fixes.impact: Your limp rate nearly doubled. This is the single biggest behavioral change.

immediate_fixes.action: Raise more preflop — restore PFR to 21%+
immediate_fixes.impact: You went from raising to limping/calling. Raise or fold.

immediate_fixes.action: C-bet the flop more when you are PFR
immediate_fixes.impact: You stopped following through on your preflop aggression.
Bet more for value, add more bluffs, stop check-calling.
2am alone cost you €6,029. Late night = tired = bad decisions.

session_management.detail: After losing >€50 in a session, take a break before next session. Currently avg -€12.54 after big losses (tilt indicator).

session_management.action: Avoid Thursdays or play shorter sessions
session_management.detail: Thursday is -17.65 bb/100, your worst day by far.

session_management.action: Dont move up stakes during downswings
session_management.detail: Moving up costs avg -€5.59/session. Stay at your level when running bad.

good_period_targets._description: These are your stats from the good period. Use them as targets to get back to that level.

dashboard_layout._instructions_for_copilot: Build a React dashboard with dark theme (background #0a0e17). Use JetBrains Mono for numbers/stats, and a clean sans-serif for body text. Color coding: green #22c55e for good/in-range, yellow #f59e0b for warning, red #ef4444 for bad/out-of-range. All data is in the "data" section of this file. The dashboard should have a sidebar navigation with tabs. Make it responsive. Use recharts for all charts.

sections.description: Big P&L number with bb/100 and rake. Show total P&L in large red text, bb/100 below, and total rake paid.
6 stat boxes in a 3x2 grid: VPIP, PFR, 3-Bet, AF, WTSD, W. Each box shows the value, GTO range below, and color-coded border (green if in range, yellow if close, red if far).

Line chart of cumulative P&L by week. X-axis = week, Y-axis = cumulative €. Draw a vertical dashed line at July 14 (period split). Color the good period green-ish, bad period red-ish. Show the P&L value on hover.
Table/rows showing each position (BTN, CO, HJ, UTG, SB, BB) with VPIP, PFR, Limp%, 3-Bet%, and bb/100. Color-code each stat vs GTO ranges. Show bb/100 in green if positive, red if negative.
Two side-by-side cards. Left: monthly P&L (bar chart, green/red bars). Right: stakes P&L table with hands, P&L, bb/100.
Two cards side by side. Left = Good Period (green border): dates, hands, P&L, bb/100. Right = Bad Period (red border): same stats. Show the difference prominently.

A table comparing every stat between good and bad periods. Columns: Stat Name | Good | Bad | Diff | Arrow. Highlight rows where the difference is large. Group by Preflop / Postflop. This is the most important table — it shows exactly what changed.

For each position (BTN through BB), show a mini card with Good vs Bad values for VPIP, PFR, Limp%, 3-Bet%. Use arrows to show direction of change. Highlight positions with biggest degradation (UTG, HJ, CO limp rates).
List of key changes as colored cards (critical=red, major=yellow, notable=blue). Each shows the stat, old value, new value, and a note. Sort by severity.
A "targets" panel showing the good-period stats as goals to return to. Format as a checklist or reference card with green values. Title: "Your Winning Form — Targets to Hit".

Stack of leak cards. Each card has: severity badge (critical/major/minor), title, your value vs GTO range (visualized as a gauge or range bar), description, and fix recommendation. Color-code by severity.
Green-bordered card listing what the player does well. Show as bullet points with checkmarks.
Numbered priority list of fixes. Each item shows priority number, action, target stat, and expected impact. Separate section for session management tips.
For each position, show stat boxes for VPIP, PFR, 3-Bet, Open Raise, Limp. Each box shows your value, the GTO range, and color-coding. This is a large section — use collapsible position panels.
Grid of postflop stats vs GTO: C-Bet Flop, C-Bet Turn, Fold to C-Bet, Check-Raise, Donk Bet, WTSD, W, AF. Each as a stat box with your value and GTO range.
Detailed preflop table by position showing: RFI, Cold Call, Fold vs Raise, Fold to 3-Bet, Call 3-Bet, 4-Bet, Iso Raise, Overlimp, Squeeze. This is the advanced view for serious study.

Three columns: Flop | Turn | River. Each shows: bets, raises, calls, folds as a horizontal stacked bar. Then key percentages below: c-bet%, check-raise%, fold-to-bet%, probe%. Show raw counts in tooltips.
When hero checks and faces a bet: show check-raise% / check-call% / check-fold% as a donut chart. Also show c-bet IP vs OOP as separate bars.
River-specific stats: how often hero bets first, fold-to-river-bet%, call%, raise%. Compare good period vs bad period river fold-to-bet (66.8% vs 69.3%).
Table showing per-position: flops seen, c-bet%, fold-to-cbet%, WTSD%, W%.

Table sorted by play rate: hand category (premium, strong_ace, ds_rundown, etc.), times dealt, times played, play rate %, average P&L per hand in bb, total P&L in bb. Color the avg P&L green if positive, red if negative. Add a bar chart showing total P&L by category.
Text card explaining: premium hands +1.30 bb avg, suited-only -0.80 bb avg, trash -2.60 bb avg. "You should fold more suited-only hands and all trash hands."
Card showing total limps, limps by position (pie chart), and average P&L per limped hand (-0.51 bb). Message: "Every limp costs you half a big blind on average."

Sortable table with all 42 opponents (5K+ hands). Columns: Name, Hands, VPIP%, PFR%, AF, 3-Bet%, C-Bet%, WTSD%, Hero P&L vs (€), Hero bb/100 vs. Default sort by hands descending. Allow sorting by any column. Color hero P&L green/red.
Click on an opponent to see a detail card: player type classification (nit/TAG/LAG/fish), all stats, hero P&L, and exploitation tip. Top 5 worst matchups highlighted in red, top 5 best in green.
Scatter plot: X = VPIP%, Y = AF. Each dot is an opponent, sized by hands played. Hero dot highlighted. Shows where hero sits vs the field (hero is the loosest player at 41.3% VPIP).
Key session stats: total sessions, win rate %, avg winning/losing session, biggest win/loss, max losing streak. Show as stat boxes.
Bar chart showing P&L by day of week. Friday is green (only winning day +1.94 bb/100), Thursday is darkest red (-17.65 bb/100). Show bb/100 and total €.
Heatmap or bar chart showing P&L by hour (0-23). Red = losing hours (especially 2am), green = winning hours (midnight, afternoon). Show total € and hands for each hour.

Three cards: Performance after big loss (>€50), after big win (>€50), after normal session. Show avg P&L per next session. Highlight that after big losses, avg is -€12.54 (tilt indicator) vs -€1.09 after normal.
Bar chart: short (1-20 hands), medium (21-100), long (100+). Show count and avg P&L for each. Helps identify optimal session length.
Three cards: 1 table, 2-3 tables, 4+ tables. Show sessions, hands, total P&L for each. Most money lost on 4+ tables.
Three cards: moved up, moved down, stayed same. Show avg P&L per session. Moving up costs -€5.59/session.
Two sections: Concerning trends (red text, bullet points) and Stable stats (green text). Each trend is a single sentence.
Full weekly P&L curve (same as overview but larger). Show cumulative line with period shading. Add a moving average line (4-week). Mark the worst weeks with red dots.
Line charts showing how key stats changed month to month: VPIP, PFR, Limp%, AF. Use the monthly data to show the degradation over time. Each stat as a small sparkline.
sections.note: You may need to compute monthly stat breakdowns from the period data — good period covers Apr-mid Jul, bad covers mid Jul-Oct.
